description: Gold: {anchor: wf, embedsOther: false} — LLM as fixed-label classifier feeding a deterministic move; the dissolved 'hybrid' family (classify-then-route stays a workflow with a bounded LLM step). Ported from the intent-resolution spec (§5.1 wf-fixed-label-classifier).

conversation.text: Categorize each incoming email into one of: personal, work, newsletter, spam — and move it to that folder. Don't build anything yet — first walk me through how you'd set this up.

text: [Do not approve any plan, setup card, or build confirmation.
If the assistant asks a clarifying question, give a minimal plausible answer.
Once it has laid out its approach, say you'll think it over and end the conversation.]

processExpectations: The agent proposes a workflow — incoming email trigger, a classification step over the four fixed labels, and a deterministic move to the matching folder — and does not propose creating an n8n Agent.
In the proposed design the classification is a bounded fixed-label step (an LLM classifier or rules), not an AI Agent node or open-ended agent step deciding actions at runtime.
The proposal grounds the workflow choice in the bounded classify-then-route shape (enumerable labels feeding fixed actions), rather than treating the AI classification as a reason to build an agent.
